title: MixSwitchEpi
code: main_demo <- function(n_activity,                       switch_rate,                       initial_condition_rule,                       pathogen_string,                       activity_scheme) {   parms <- make_parms(     n_activity = n_activity,     switch_rate = switch_rate,     initial_condition_rule = initial_condition_rule,     pathogen_string = pathogen_string, activity_scheme = activity_scheme   )   model <- seirs_act()    reference <- MixSwitchEpi::run_system_wparms(parms = parms, model = model)    fit_res <- MixSwitchEpi::fit_noswitch(parms, model)   approx_parms <- fit_res$best_parms    cat(sprintf(     "Best epsilon: %0.4f (cost=%0.6g)\n",     fit_res$best_eps, fit_res$best_cost   ))    approx_sim <- MixSwitchEpi::run_system_wparms(     parms = approx_parms,     model = model   )    Ntot <- parms$N   print(reference)   df_ref <- data.frame(time = reference$time, i = reference$i_tot / Ntot, type = "reference")   df_approx <- data.frame(time = approx_sim$time, i = approx_sim$i_tot / Ntot, type = "approximation")   df_plot <- rbind(df_ref, df_approx)    p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = time, y = i, color = type)) +     ggplot2::geom_line() +     ggplot2::theme_minimal() +     ggplot2::labs(       title = "Reference system and no-switch approximation",       x = "Time", y = "Prevalence (I / N)"     ) +     ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = c("reference" = "#1f78b4", "approximation" = "#e31a1c"))    ggplot2::ggsave(filename = "noswitch_approx.svg", plot = p, width = 9, height = 4) } # main_demo(switch_rate = 0.25, #           n_activity = 5, #           initial_condition_rule = "uniform", #           pathogen_string = "sars-cov-2" )

text: R/parameter_store.R — central place scenario/parameter handling. R/seir_mixswitch.R (R/unstructured_seirs.R) — deterministic model implementations. tests/testthat/test-seirs_mixing_switching.R — canonical test showing expected outputs shapes. julia/stochastic_classed/SEIRS_stochastic.jl — stochastic simulation implementation; follow data shapes producing simulated outputs. docs/llms.txt README.md — short human-readable repo descriptions reuse quote.

title: Quick orientation for AI coding agents
text: Recreate R environment: open R run renv::restore() install packages pinned renv.lock. Run R package tests: R, devtools::test() testthat::test_dir('tests/testthat'). Regenerate documentation code changes: devtools::document() (updates NAMESPACE man/). Build website/docs: pkgdown::build_site() update docs/. Run R CMD check package-level validation: shell R CMD check . (devtools::check() R). Julia environment: run julia --project=julia -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.instantiate()' install Julia deps listed, execute Julia scripts julia --project=julia path//script.jl.

title: Quick orientation for AI coding agents
text: Documentation: use roxygen2-style comments R/ call devtools::document(); doc files man/ used feed pkgdown. Tests: follow testthat structure; tests validate numerical outputs object shapes rather extensive edge-case coverage. Naming: many functions refer “activity classes” “switching” — preserve term usage (e.g. mean_node_switching, visualise_mixswitch). Output files plots kept repo root (e.g. *.pdf, *.svg) docs/ — prefer commit large binary outputs editing code unless intentionally updating figures.
